success_factors: Workflow status is success with all planned nodes executed (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Import-first adapter strategy matched repository characteristics (Python API with high import feasibility 0.86).
MCP plugin service started healthy over stdio, indicating baseline runtime viability.
Repository structure was successfully discovered despite zip fallback ingestion.
No runtime errors or warnings were emitted by the pipeline.
failure_reasons: Quality gaps remain: original project tests were not validated (passed=false with empty details), and generated tool metadata is inconsistent (tool_endpoints=0 vs non-empty endpoint list).
DeepWiki augmentation failed, reducing depth of automated semantic analysis.
Endpoint namespace collisions (e.g., repeated run/model/statepoint) indicate generation defects that can cause ambiguity.

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully; exact per-node timing not provided. Zip fallback likely added moderate overhead but did not block execution.
node_performance.analysis_time: Completed successfully across a large codebase (658 files scanned); complexity/risk extraction was adequate.
node_performance.generation_time: Completed successfully and produced core plugin scaffolding (7 files), but generated LOC/size metrics were zero, suggesting instrumentation/reporting gaps.
node_performance.test_time: MCP plugin health check passed quickly; however, no substantive functional smoke/integration tests were executed.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Undetermined from telemetry (reported 0 MB indicates missing measurement, not true zero usage).
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: Undetermined from telemetry (0% suggests metrics not captured).
resource_usage.disk_usage: Low generated footprint (7 files), but reported size=0 indicates incomplete metric collection.
